item.title: Thursday Finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Dogs hold on to down Canberra in brutal wet-weather battle
item.description: It was far from easy, but Canterbury have held on for a narrow win over the Raiders in torrential conditions in Canberra.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle downgraded to category 2 as west coast communities warned to stay in place - as it happened
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle downgraded to a category 2

item.title: China cracks down on illegal fentanyl trade after Trump delays Beijing meeting
item.description: The country is one of the world’s highest producers of the chemicals used to produce the synthetic opioid in America.

item.title: BTS's new comeback album, concert, Netflix documentary: What you need to know
item.description: K-pop supergroup BTS makes a comeback from mandatory military service today with a new album, concert and documentary. Here's why that's a big deal.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: History-making Oscars moments, a royal visit and fans storm the NRL field
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Prime minister's department requests modelling for gas and coal tax to shield Australia from impact of war
item.description: The prime minister's department has requested options to impose a new tax on gas giants to help shield Australians from the economic shock of the Middle East war.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle downgraded to a category two
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle has been downgraded as it moves across Cape York Peninsula, but authorities warn it could dump half a metre of rain in Far North Queensland, even as it continues to weaken.

item.title: Migrants say new laws stopping temporary migration from war-torn countries 'a slap in the face'
item.description: Migrants and human rights advocates say new laws stopping temporary migration from designated countries will keep families apart and make Australia's immigration system less predictable and fair.

item.title: How is the Middle East war affecting Australian superannuation funds?
item.description: Experts are urging Australians to stay calm and weather the storm as the effects of the war in the Middle East reverberate through financial markets.

item.title: Rideshare and delivery drivers rethink gig economy amid sky-high petrol prices
item.description: Rideshare and delivery platforms face mounting pressure to bump up rates for drivers as they struggle with skyrocketing fuel prices.

item.title: Jim Chalmers is trying to use the current crisis as a springboard for reform
item.description: If the treasurer is serious about budget repair the government has to avoid the inevitable pressure for more cost-of-living relief which will only grow as many families' budgets become unmanageable.

item.title: Raiders prop Josh Papali'i facing NRL suspension, glass shatters at Canberra Stadium injuring coach
item.description: Canberra Raiders prop Josh Papali'i is facing a one-match suspension for a high tackle during his side's 14-10 loss to the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs on Thursday night.

item.title: The Matildas' last Asian Cup final at home shows how far they've come in 20 years
item.description: The last time the Matildas played an Asian Cup final in Australia, there were 5,000 people in the stands at a suburban football ground in Adelaide.

item.title: Australian Opal Centre at Lightning Ridge runs out of money before construction is complete
item.description: The Australian Opal Centre in Lightning Ridge risks becoming a "white elephant" as construction indefinitely stalls and an auditor raises concerns about the organisation's viability.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
item.description: The ABC's Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran is in Beirut and providing daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: We might have just found where Australia's red line is with Donald Trump
item.description: The war in the Middle East is heaping pressure on Australia's relationship with the US and we could be about to see if that comes with consequences.

item.title: Sydney independent service station, taxi drivers hit hard by rising petrol and diesel prices
item.description: The fuel crisis is squeezing independently owned service stations, with some being forced to sell fuel at above-average prices, even though their margins are near zero or below cost.

item.title: Maggie Beer brings aged care nutrition project to outback renal health hub
item.description: Culinary icon Maggie Beer and her team are working with an Alice Springs organisation that provides dialysis and aged care on Indigenous homelands.

item.title: Government to continue subsidising Australia's two remaining oil refineries — as it happened
item.description: The federal government will continue subsidising Australia's only two remaining oil refineries amid the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Developer Chris Pinzone dropped from Paul Pisasale corruption probe, embroiled in farm payments dispute
item.description: Developer Chris Pinzone, who was central to former Ipswich mayor Paul Pisasale's corruption case, has been pursued in a number of civil cases.

item.title: As it happened -- Major bank says oil price could soar to $US150
item.description: The CBA says there is a "strong likelihood" of the oil price reaching $US120‑150 per barrel.

item.title: Rare Rymer apples found on NSW Southern Tablelands farm
item.description: The Rymer variety of cooking apple, once believed to have died out, has been found on a rural property in the NSW Southern Tablelands.

item.title: AFL investigates after Hawthorn's Nick Watson accuses Sydney fans of throwing drink bottle
item.description: Hawthorn's Nick Watson says Sydney Swans supporters threw drink bottles at him after the Hawks defeated the Swans on Thursday night.

item.title: Calls for urgent action on flood mitigation for Alice Springs
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Urgent calls for flood mitigation in Alice Springs, as insurance premiums skyrocket
item.description: A shock flash flooding event in Alice Springs last month saw more than 100 millimetres of rain fall on the catchment in a matter of hours. The deluge has reignited calls for urgent action on flood mitigation in the outback town, as insurance companies calculate the risk with eye-watering premiums.

item.title: Townsville truckie wants action on fuel as Queensland prices hit record high
item.description: Almost everything arrives in outback Australia by truck. Truckies say record-high fuel prices will have to be passed on.

item.title: Numbulwar evacuated as other NT communities prepare to shelter from Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Ahead of Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle's expected impact on the eastern Top End, about 500 people from one community have been evacuated to Darwin, while others prepare to "shelter in place".

item.title: Fuel and freight woes from Middle East conflict could end some carrot farms in WA, warns industry body
item.description: Shipping disruptions due to conflict in the Middle East mean Western Australian carrot growers have lost access to their main customer.

item.title: What's a library worth? Report puts dollar figure on 'invaluable' institutions
item.description: Every dollar invested in the State Library of NSW is worth $4.25 in community benefits, according to a new report commissioned by the library.

item.title: How to choose sheets and bed linen that last as long as possible
item.description: Shopping for sheets can be overwhelming and expensive. This is what textile experts say to look out for.

item.title: Albanese, Burke shouted at as anger erupts at Eid prayers at mosque
item.description: An event to mark the end of Ramadan was disrupted by a protest against the prime minister and home affairs minister, who were visiting the mosque in Western Sydney.

item.title: Historic flour mill wall in Mount Gambier will be destroyed despite community opposition
item.description: The remnants of a 140-year-old historic flour mill will be demolished to make way for a housing development despite a regional community calling for the city's history to be preserved.

item.title: Iran war drives up cost of living in Asia, hits crucial tech industries
item.description: The Middle East war triggers everything from fuel rationing in Sri Lanka to a helium supply crisis for Asia's crucial semiconductor industries.

item.title: White House meeting with Japan's PM turns awkward as Trump jokes about Pearl Harbor
item.description: When he was asked why the US did not notify allies like Japan ahead of the strikes on Iran, Donald Trump made a quip drawing a parallel with the Japanese air raid in 1941.

item.title: Video shows news crew narrowly avoiding Israeli strike in southern Lebanon
item.description: An English-language Russian state broadcaster has accused Israel of deliberately attacking its news crew in southern Lebanon. IDF says it will investigate.

item.title: The ABC is investigating aged care again and we want to hear from you
item.description: We're launching an investigation asking you — staff, insiders, those navigating the aged care system and their supporters — to help the ABC understand if the government's promise of reform has been delivered.

item.title: How the 'four right blokes' turned the ABC's coverage of the VFA into must-watch TV for footy fans
item.description: As Aussie Rules was forced to reckon with a national competition, four men helped make the VFA must-watch TV on the ABC for Victorian footy fans.

item.title: When girls don't feel comfortable or confident in their uniforms it can cause them to drop out of sport
item.description: Studies in Australia and overseas show a big percentage of girls drop out of sport when they become teenagers. One of the contributing factors is the clothes they have to wear.

item.title: What to watch, read and listen to this weekend: New James Blake and a sun-drenched crime drama
item.description: This weekend go down the rabbit hole with a podcast about a Canadian cult or discover James Blake's latest sonic salve.

item.title: NT cattle station Aroona sells to Argentinian family for $44 million
item.description: An Argentinian family has expanded its portfolio of NT cattle stations with the purchase of Aroona Station.

item.title: Teenager Moise Kouame makes history with win over Zachary Svajda at ATP Masters Miami Open
item.description: Teenager Moise Kouame steals the spotlight as Australians Alexei Popyrin and James Duckworth crash out of the multi-million-dollar Miami Open.

item.title: Poverty increased after Victoria 2022 floods while disposable income decreased, report finds
item.description: Poverty and unemployment rose after Victoria's 2022 floods, with those in inundated areas experiencing lower disposable income than those who weren't affected, according to a new report.

item.title: Ashton Hurn lives in hope of becoming SA's premier after election
item.description: Polls predict the Liberals are heading for a wipe-out in South Australia on Saturday, but party leader Ashton Hurn says she is still working with the goal of becoming the state's leader rather than analysing what went wrong.

item.title: One Nation candidate Aoi Baxter wanted in the UK for failing to attend court on sexual touching charge
item.description: South Australian One Nation candidate Aoi Baxter has a warrant for his arrest in the UK after he failed to show up to court to face a charge of sexually touching a woman without consent.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle leaves trail of damage as residents shelter in boats, shipping containers, WWII bunker
item.description: Far North Queensland residents say Tropical Cyclone Narelle has left a trail of damage across Cape York.

item.title: Trump has postponed his meeting with Xi, but China is not wasting any time on it
item.description: Donald Trump has postponed a meeting with Xi Jinping because he is busy with Iran and the Middle East — and that's good news for China.

item.title: Alana King stakes claim for T20 World Cup recall with dominant performance against West Indies
item.description: Alana King gives Australia's selectors a timely reminder of the quality she possesses, helping secure a win against the West Indies.

item.title: Dorothy Day House raided by police over 'From the River to the Sea' banner
item.description: Officers raid Dorothy Day House in Greenslopes over a banner with the slogan "From the River to the Sea", which is now a prohibited expression in Queensland.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Japan coach talks favouritism ahead of final against Matildas
item.description: Japan's coach says determining a favourite for the Women's Asian Cup final between his team and Australia is a matter for bees and flies.

item.title: Epworth releases details into hospital review following endometriosis allegations
item.description: A South Australian health commissioner will lead the review of clinical governance at Epworth Hospital, after allegations a top gynaecologist performed unnecessary operations to remove endometriosis.

item.title: Federal government approves 1,695 gas wells in rural Queensland
item.description: A major multinational gas venture has just secured federal approval to ramp up extraction in rural Queensland — locking in operations for the next 55 years.

item.title: Bellamy pinpoints Storm's downfall as Broncos achieve 10-year first
item.description: Storm coach Craig Bellamy identifies the area in which he believes his side let themselves down, as the Broncos celebrate a rare win in Melbourne.

item.title: Katherine residents brace for second major flood in two weeks as Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle approaches NT
item.description: Just two weeks after its worst flood in almost 30 years, Katherine businesses are being sandbagged and a field hospital has been set up as locals brace for another major weather event.

item.title: Coroner finds paramedic failures in death of Phillip Mark Walton in custody
item.description: A coroner has found paramedics failed to meet standards of care for a man who died from cardiac arrhythmia while being restrained by police.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Bulldogs 'tick off' another item on 2026 AFL season bucket list
item.description: A brilliant Oskar Baker goal clinches a thrilling win for the Bulldogs over the Crows, leaving Luke Beveridge's side unbeaten after three matches.

item.title: ABC meteorologist Tom Saunders on the forecast for Cyclone Narelle over the Northern Territory.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chuck Norris, film star and martial artist, dies aged 86 from sudden illness
item.description: Norris's epic fight with kung fu superstar Bruce Lee in The Way of The Dragon helped him become an icon, and he went on to star in more than 20 movies.

item.title: Politics is about to become all about gas
item.description: Scott Morrison's assertion that the chase to secure vaccines in early 2021 was "not a race" cost him dearly. A similar global contest is already underway, only this time it involves securing energy supplies.

item.title: From pub knitting to wonky ceramics, arts and crafts are having a resurgence
item.description: Craft trends come and go, but a resurgence in social arts and crafts is proving that creativity and human connection are still very much in vogue.

item.title: Greig Fraser filmed the unfilmable for Dune. Now he's back for Project Hail Mary
item.description: With most of Project Hail Mary's action taking place in far off corners of space it was up to Australian cinematographer Greig Fraser to create what we've never seen.

item.title: In the Strait of Hormuz, Iran unleashes a plot against the West years in the making
item.description: By moving so much of the war into the Strait of Hormuz and Persian Gulf, Tehran has hit its adversaries where it hurts the most and analysts warn that while the United States military is mighty, history shows in asymmetrical warfare, the lesser power can win the day.

item.title: Government leaves door open to gas, coal tax as companies warn it will threaten supply
item.description: The Greens and One Nation are among those that want gas profits levied, with pressure mounting on Labor to respond to growing calls for reform.

item.title: Coroner finds Worimi woman's death at Lightning Ridge hospital was preventable
item.description: The coroner found that a mother of three could have been saved if a transfer from a remote New South Wales hospital had been ordered sooner.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: Where is it forecast to hit in the NT?
item.description: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle and see live wind speeds as the system travels reaches land in the Northern Territory.

item.title: Peak fashion body aims to rebuild local manufacturing battered by offshore supply chain interruptions
item.description: Australia's peak clothing and textile organisation launches a national strategy to revitalise an industry hit by years of turmoil.

item.title: The government says fuel supply is safe until mid-April. What happens after that?
item.description: The government is responding to fuel shortages due to the war in the Middle East and says Australia has enough supply until mid-April. Here's what experts say could happen after that.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle dumps hundreds of millimetres of rain, cuts power, communities on flood watch
item.description: Hundreds of millimetres of rain drench parts of Far North Queensland, with authorities urging residents to remain on flood watch as Tropical Cyclone Narelle recovery efforts begin.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of 2000s hip-hop, Mercury in retrograde and disturbing kids' TV puppets
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: The Snowy Mountains mystery of the Southern Cloud aviation disaster
item.description: When eight people vanished without a trace in 1931, Australia's first major airline disaster became a 27-year mystery that gripped the nation, until a hiker's chance discovery changed everything.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Young Japanese-Australians showing up loud and proud for final
item.description: Young Japanese diaspora in Sydney will be showing up loud and proud in support of Japan against Australia's Matildas at the Asian Cup final on Saturday.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle impacting NT coastal areas, overnight landfall predicted — as it happened
item.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is impacting coastal areas in the north-eastern Northern Territory, with the system expected to cross the coast around 3am Sunday before continuing to track west across the territory.

item.title: Space junk is cluttering Earth's low orbit with high-velocity projectiles and it's getting worse
item.description: Thousands of satellites are being launched into space at low Earth orbit, joining hundreds of thousands of pieces of space junk, and it's becoming a real problem.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: When do the Matildas play Japan in the final? How to watch and follow the decider
item.description: The Matildas' quest for the Women's Asian Cup trophy reaches its climax tonight, when they face Japan at Sydney's Stadium Australia. Here's how to watch and follow the game.

item.title: Australia's Nicola Olyslagers wins silver at World Athletics Indoor Championships in Poland
item.description: Australia's two-time defending champion Nicola Olyslagers reaches the podium in the women's high jump at the World Athletics Indoor Championships in Poland.

item.title: New Whitsundays Ngaro Track hiking trail fuels debate over tourism in national parks
item.description: With only 15 walkers a day and costing $150 per person, the new Ngaro Track sparks debate about commercial tourism in Queensland's national parks.

item.title: Western New South Wales farmers bear brunt of fuel redistribution
item.description: Scott Worthington has run out of fuel, after first running out of water on his drought-stricken farm, and watching his pastures turn to dirt.

item.title: Why I watch Married at First Sight with my teenage son
item.description: Watching the largely fake, but also completely true, juggernaut reality TV series Married at First Sight with our 14-year-old was never a deliberate choice but it is potentially very powerful.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: How the goldfish mentality is helping Matildas defender Kaitlyn Torpey
item.description: A Ted Lasso lesson, and an Australian indie rock classic, are inspiring Kaitlyn Torpey to take her career to new heights, as she prepares for tonight's Women's Asian Cup final.

item.title: Warning system upgrades to come amid Northern Territory flood emergencies
item.description: The Northern Territory and federal governments promise to improve flood warning systems after flood-ravaged communities say they were put at risk during rushed evacuations.

item.title: Polls close in South Australian election, after record number of early votes
item.description: Voting has closed for South Australia's 2026 state election, where One Nation could threaten the Liberal Party's status as the official opposition and Labor is expected to win a landslide victory.

item.title: Lyons on Trump's calls for countries that use the Strait of Hormuz to 'get involved'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle to hit NT as category three on Saturday night, affects to begin this afternoon
item.description: After regaining strength in the gulf, Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is expected to make landfall in the Northern Territory on Saturday evening.

item.title: Pioneering Auslan program breaks down barriers in Canberra schoolyard
item.description: Mount Rogers Primary School in Canberra is the only public school in the ACT to teach Australian Sign Language as its second language.

item.title: Farmers count the cost of floods, with the industry warning of produce shortages and higher prices
item.description: The farming industry is warning consumers that multiple weather disasters in Queensland, paired with high fuel costs, will likely limit fresh produce supply and push prices higher.

item.title: Is the 'big spike' in public transport usage in Perth because of high fuel prices?
item.description: WA Premier Roger Cook says public transport usage has risen over the past three weeks, over the same period that fuel prices have skyrocketed, but it's not clear whether one increase is related to the other.

item.title: Thousands told to evacuate as Hawaii's O'ahu island hit by severe floods
item.description: Thousands of residents in Hawaii have been warned to evacuate towns north of Honolulu as floodwaters inundate streets and damage homes.

item.title: Elders in Kalumburu revitalise endangered Indigenous languages
item.description: Three Indigenous languages are spoken by a handful of people in the remote community of Kalumburu — now efforts are underway to revive them and teach the next generation.

item.title: 'A bad look': Docker under fire for taunting Demons oppponent
item.description: Fremantle's Patrick Voss may face scrutiny after he taunted Melbourne's Harrison Petty during the Dockers' 48-point win in Perth.

item.title: Easy meatballs in rich tomato sauce are a versatile crowd-pleaser - ABC News
item.description: If you are after an easy and delicious dinner to serve a few or a crowd, these meatballs in rich tomato sauce are perfect to scale up or down and perfect simply with bread and salad.

item.title: Tigers halves pair set for scans after limping off in loss to Rabbitohs
item.description: Wests Tigers captain Jarome Luai is expected to be out of action for several weeks, while his halves partner is also in doubt after the duo limped off during their 20-16 loss to South Sydney.

item.title: Resurgence of ancient trade as young people seek to slow down in a fast-paced world
item.description: Carving wooden spoons isn't a hobby you hear much about, but it's one of many traditional crafts being revived as young people look for ways to slow down and get away from their screens.

item.title: Albanese defends Australia’s response to the Middle East War
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dragons coach Shane Flanagan reveals return date for Loko Pasifiki Tonga following hospital discharge
item.description: Young St George Illawarra prop Loko Pasifiki Tonga is expected to return to play just a few weeks after being hospitalised with a neck injury.

item.title: Rhoda Roberts AO, Indigenous leader in arts, culture and media, dies aged 66
item.description: Rhoda Roberts AO, the arts executive who had an enduring impact on the cultural life of this country, has died. The Widjabul Wieybal woman was the creative force behind Australia's best-loved festivals and helped bring Welcomes to Country to the modern era.

item.title: Woman injured in tractor accident at CMC Rocks music festival in Willowbank
item.description: The accident happened near Ipswich at CMC Rocks, which bills itself as the premier country music festival in Australia.

item.title: As it happened: Matildas fall agonisingly short after defeat to Japan
item.description: The Matildas have fallen to a heartbreaking Women's Asian Cup final defeat, losing 1-0 to Japan despite relentless pressure in the final quarter of the match.

item.title: Surfer in rough East Arnhem Land swell ahead of TC Narelle
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Labor Party retains power in South Australia, as One Nation vote surges
item.description: The Labor Party has been re-elected in South Australia, while One Nation appears to have achieved its best electoral result anywhere in the country in nearly 30 years.

item.title: How are the results tracking in your SA electorate? Take a look at the map of your seat
item.description: Explore the results across metropolitan Adelaide and regional South Australia with these maps as votes continue to be counted.

item.title: The ABC's Olivana Lathouris speaks to Katherine local member Jo Hersey about TC Narelle preparations.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: From a 'mountain to climb' to over a cliff, SA Liberals face total wipeout
item.description: Ashton Hurn said she had a "mountain to climb" when she took on the role of SA Liberal leader late last year. Now, the party's primary vote has fallen off a cliff, a result sure to see Liberals around the country asking why.

item.title: Groote Broadcasting's radio manager Percy Bishop gives an update as TC Narelle nears.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Australia thwarted by wonder goal as golden moment goes begging — Matildas vs Japan quick hits
item.description: The Matildas are stunned by a miracle strike and a final chance goes begging on a momentous, defeating night for Australian football. Here are the quick hits from the Asian Cup final.

item.title: Albanese government's 'don't panic buy' fuel message doesn't work
item.description: As costs soar, queues form and regional bowsers run dry, the government must ensure its talking points are not detached from the incentives people are actually responding to.

item.title: A journey into my family's past taught me to keep sight of the present
item.description: Genealogy is an addictive hobby but unlocking family secrets — and dealing with the fallout — can change your life.

item.title: Meet the Australians making new games for the Game Boy console in 2026
item.description: A small community of game developers is releasing new games for Nintendo's decades-old video game console, harnessing the nostalgia of these classic devices.

item.title: Meet the women making their mark in the shearing industry
item.description: These women have found a community in an unlikely place — the male-dominated shearing sheds. From wool-classers to rouseabouts, this is how they play their vital part.

item.title: Three sailors remain missing in the Strait of Hormuz. Their families are hoping for a 'miracle'
item.description: Three people are unaccounted for after a merchant ship, trying to escape the war zone via the Strait of Hormuz, was bombed by Iran. Their loved ones are hoping against hope.

item.title: It's February 2020 all over again, but this time one man can end it
item.description: Though few Australians recognise it, our economic institutions have an exceptional track record of making good decisions in a crisis, but the uncertainty of Trump's war is a new type of challenge.

item.title: What is stagflation and what can we do about it?
item.description: As the Iran war dries up oil and gas supplies, should we prepare for stagflation?

item.title: The price of Australia's 'ethical' surrogacy laws
item.description: Blind spots in surrogacy laws have been pushing desperate Australians overseas to get a baby, which comes with its own risks. Is it time to change our views on paying for surrogacy?

item.title: Where Australians spend their money and feel a budget pinch
item.description: As the cost-of-living crisis intensifies, people from all walks of life are struggling. ABC Your Say asked ordinary Australians how they were making ends meet.

item.title: Trump gives Iran 48-hour deadline to open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump is warning the US will "obliterate" Iran’s power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened within 48 hours.

item.title: One Nation's moment arrives at SA's election. It might only be the beginning
item.description: If it can happen in South Australia, it can happen anywhere.That's the takeaway from One Nation's performance at this state election.

item.title: Gulf states and energy markets become 'soft belly' of US-Israel war with Iran
item.description: It appears that by sparking Iran's escalating strikes on Qatar, Israel may have crossed somewhat of a red line for Trump.

item.title: Cape York communities begin clean-up and recovery after Tropical Cyclone Narelle
item.description: As ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle crosses the Northern Territory, communities in Far North Queensland are counting the cost and cleaning up, hopeful power and reception will be restored soon.

item.title: Scientists close to confirming Port Phillip Bay banjo shark breeding location
item.description: Researchers are tracking more than 100 sharks and rays in Melbourne's bay to assess how they're affected by climate change and human behaviour.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas endure familiar pain, but will the relief ever come?
item.description: The Matildas were despondent after losing the Asian Cup final to Japan for the third time, as the golden generation's shot at a major trophy may have passed.

item.title: Narelle downgraded as NT braces for flood, WA enters warning zone — as it happened
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle has been downgraded to a tropical low as it moves across the Northern Territory towards Western Australia.

item.title: How the US govt used 'white supremacist' material and memes to hire an army of ICE agents
item.description: The White House faces scrutiny over posts experts describe as "flat out white supremacist" amid calls for oversight after ICE agents left "death cards" in immigrants' cars.

item.title: Does an acid attack in Indonesia signal its democratic unravelling?
item.description: An acid attack that seriously burned an Indonesian human rights campaigner reflects a pattern of authoritarian resurgence, in which any form of confrontational activism is viewed as a threat to the state.

item.title: Antarctica's only 'Hilton' is a former radio shack at abandoned Wilkes station
item.description: There are hundreds of Hilton hotels around the world, but none are quite like the Wilkes "Hilton" in Antarctica — a small rustic hut used by Australian expeditioners.

item.title: High-powered e-bike use among children prompts call for more action to stop 'preventable injuries'
item.description: Bans on children riding e-mobility devices are being considered by several states, but a frontline surgeon says more must be done to stop "preventable injuries".

item.title: Tasmania Devils fans get first taste of victory at VFL clash with Coburg
item.description: In front a sold-out crowd, the Tasmania Devils have won their first VFL game in 18 years, as they work towards their entry to the AFL.

item.title: From rhinos to sheep dogs: How zookeeper Scott Smith became a champion dog trainer
item.description: Scott Smith spends his days caring for black rhinos at Dubbo's Taronga Western Plains Zoo but, after a long day at work, he swaps wild animals for a smaller domestic variety.

item.title: Robert Mueller, special counsel who probed but did not charge Trump, dies at 81
item.description: Robert Mueller, the no-nonsense former FBI chief who documented Russia's interference in the 2016 US election and its contacts with Donald Trump's campaign — but opted not to bring criminal charges against a sitting president — dies aged 81.

item.title: Regional commuters take extra jobs and use credit to afford fuel as prices surge
item.description: The rising cost of fuel is forcing drivers across the country to make difficult decisions, but regional residents are particularly hard hit due to the tyranny of distance and the absence of public transport.

item.title: Jessica Hull and Kurtis Marschall win bronze for Australia at World Athletics Indoor Championships
item.description: Australia adds to its medal tally in Poland, with Jessica Hull and Kurtis Marschall reaching the podium.

item.title: Outback rugby league revival unites Thargomindah community as season kicks off
item.description: More than two decades after players hung up their boots, a conversation after work sparked the revival of the Thargomindah Bulls, uniting the town in the process.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Show us your trivia smarts. Let's play, HARD!
item.description: Don't blame your poor performance on a slow-start Sunday. We all know the result would be the same on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday …

item.title: Tasmanian tiger footprints show the carnivorous marsupial once roamed South Australian coast
item.description: The discovery of ancient footprints shows the carnivorous marsupial once roamed the Eyre Peninsula coastline.

item.title: Australia's Talia Gibson defeats Naomi Osaka 7-5, 6-4 at Miami Open
item.description: Fresh from her Indian Wells quarterfinal appearance, Talia Gibson causes a boilover with her straight-sets defeat of four-time major winner Naomi Osaka in Miami.

item.title: Flying Lotus on Big Mama EP, video games, and 'big booty kaiju'
item.description: Ahead of an Australian show, the musician, filmmaker and Brainfeeder label founder talks about discovering a new process for his Big Mama EP.

item.title: Football legend Kelvin Templeton draws on 'wild and dangerous' 70s VFL in debut novel
item.description: The former Footscray and Melbourne star reflects on the once-brutal world of Australian football with the release of his novel, Collision, an exploration of the stoicism and quiet anxieties of Australian masculinity.

item.title: Six fuel ships to Australia cancelled or deferred, Bowen confirms, though gap has been filled
item.description: Energy Minister Chris Bowen says six fuel tankers that were scheduled to deliver oil to Australia next month were recently cancelled or deferred, out of about 81 ships expected from mid-April to mid-May — assuring however that supply gap has already been largely replaced.

item.title: Derby homeowner left $10,000 out of pocket by builder Stephen Keenan
item.description: Samantha Hall is chasing up the builder who owes her more than $10,000 — but she can't find him.

item.title: Father and son hiking journey 'indirectly' raising awareness for Tourette syndrome
item.description: Daniel Toole has taken a year off work to walk around the world with his 18-year-old, non-verbal son, Ashton, who is living with autism spectrum disorder and Tourette syndrome.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: Follow its path across the NT
item.description: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle and see live wind speeds as the system travels across the Northern Territory.

item.title: The do and don'ts of fuel storage, as petrol and diesel prices surge amid Iran war
item.description: With some motorists turning to stockpiling fuel, authorities caution against using unsafe or even illegal containers at the petrol pump.

item.title: What is the WA government saying about fuel disruption amid the Middle East war?
item.description: As West Australians feel the effects of skyrocketing fuel amid the Middle East conflict, the government is treading the line between raising the alarm and sensible action, writes Courtney Withers.

item.title: Australia facing 'crunch time' as oil shortages begin to hit Asian suppliers
item.description: Australia is yet to directly feel the impact of supply shortages caused by the Iran war. But that's set to change in the coming weeks, with oil supply shortages now hitting the country's fuel suppliers in Asia.

item.title: Search underway for missing man after three rescued at sea off Tasmania's north west
item.description: Tasmania Police says three people have been rescued, with a man still missing, after a fishing boat capsized in waters off Tasmania's north-west coast.

item.title: Trump threatens to put ICE agents in airport security unless Democrats end shutdown
item.description: US President Donald Trump has threatened to deploy ICE agents to US airports if congressional Democrats do not agree to fund airport safety immediately.

item.title: When is location sharing a red flag in relationships?
item.description: Experts say sharing where you are with a partner using technology should be mutual and enhance a feeling of safety.

item.title: Soaring fuel prices see spike in EV interest in Australia
item.description: As Chinese electric vehicle makers rapidly expand in Australia, interest is growing thanks to rising fuel prices.

item.title: Moment an Iranian missile hit Dimona, Israel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How the Middle East war plays into China's hands
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Cuba's Castro dynasty could shape US crisis talks as Australian diaspora awaits Trump 'takeover'
item.description: Former Cuban president Fidel Castro's enigmatic lineage could form part of Donald Trump's shadowy plans to "take" or strike a deal with the Caribbean nation.

item.title: '25 years is long enough': Scott won't ask for patience from Bombers fans
item.description: West Coast wins its first match since round 11 last year while the Bombers sink to a second straight crushing loss to start the 2026 season.

item.title: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle tracks across NT, communities brace for flooding
item.description: The Northern Territory is preparing for strong gusts and drenching rain as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle moves further into the Top End.

item.title: SA election throws a spotlight on One Nation's undercard fight that has left the Liberals wounded
item.description: Labor won the South Australian election in a landslide, but nationally, more attention has been on the undercard fight, which has left the Liberal Party deeply wounded.

item.title: Socceroos recall for Awer Mabil sends message ahead of FIFA World Cup
item.description: The recall of forgotten Socceroo Awer Mabil is proof Tony Popovic places no limit on how wide he casts his FIFA World Cup selection net, says Australia defender Jason Geria.

item.title: Anthony Albanese urges 'vigilance' against those seeking to 'turn back the clock' on Australia
item.description: Anthony Albanese says the country must be "vigilant" against politicians seeking to return to an era of Australia that was less accepting of migrants, following the South Australian election where One Nation overtook the Liberal Party on a platform opposing multiculturalism.

item.title: Eels coach calls out NRL rules after key forward suffers torn ACL
item.description: Parramatta Eels forward J'maine Hopgood suffers an ACL injury in a victory over Dragons, leaving coach Jason Ryles to lament the NRL's insistence on speeding up the game.

item.title: Alana King takes two wickets as Australia defeats West Indies in women's T20 series
item.description: Alana King adds more intrigue to Australia's spin dilemma, strengthening her T20 World Cup claims with two more wickets to save her side from an upset loss to West Indies.

item.title: Hannah Green wins Australian WPGA Championship at Sanctuary Cove Golf Club
item.description: Hannah Green's glittering run of form continues, winning the Australian WPGA Championship for a third consecutive year.

item.title: One Nation wins first seat in South Australia's lower house, ABC projects
item.description: One Nation is projected to win its first seat in South Australia's lower house, the first time the party has done so outside of Queensland.

item.title: Matildas rally behind Caitlin Foord as Sam Kerr faces club limbo after Asian Cup defeat
item.description: The Matildas have backed Caitlin Foord to overcome her Asian Cup heartache as questions continue to swirl about the future of captain Sam Kerr.

item.title: Cricket Australia releases full international schedule for the 2026/2027 summer
item.description: Cricket Australia has been forced into starting the core part of its Test summer in Perth on a Wednesday, as a result of the most congested schedule in the sport's history.

item.title: US-Israeli airstrike on Dezful, Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Banned NDIS provider filmed entering disability housing
item.description: A man banned from working in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and having any contact with the program’s participants has been filmed entering specialist disability housing in Melbourne.

item.title: Microsoft's default wallpaper Bliss, the most viewed photograph in history, turns 30 this year
item.description: Plastered across screens in offices, schools and homes globally, Bliss is one of the most well-known images in the world.

item.title: Markets are complacent as Iran war threatens global economy
item.description: The markets' benign assessment of the Iran war is wrong and the damage to the global economy could get worse.

item.title: South Australian election shows One Nation threatens Liberals and Labor
item.description: One Nation delivers its best electoral result in nearly 30 years, demonstrating that opinion polls showing its vote rising was not a political beat-up but a real electoral phenomenon that will lead to a political reckoning in Australian politics.

item.title: Home building industry facing COVID-like price spikes due to Middle East war
item.description: From sand to concrete, suppliers are bringing in "emergency fuel levies" as petrol prices soar due to the war in the Middle East. Major companies that supply pipes for plumbing are even warning of shortages.

item.title: Most Australians expect national security crises to hit home in years ahead, research finds
item.description: Threats like economic shocks and cyber attacks were considered most likely, but nearly half of Australians expect foreign military action on Australian soil.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: The Matildas need a grand plan to stave off end-of-an-era concerns
item.description: The Women's Asian Cup has shown that without a grand plan for the future of women's football and a willingness to stick to it, the Matildas may never capture an elusive major trophy again.

item.title: Why Ghanim named his robotic arms after the Peaky Blinders brothers
item.description: Ghanim Al Shnen became a pioneer in the use of mind-controlled robotic limbs after losing both his arms. Although he would never have chosen this fate, it ultimately gave him the life for which he longed.

item.title: Rising insurance costs risk turning Australian retirements into a 'prison'
item.description: The Australian retirement dream is being destabilised by the rising cost of home insurance, as retirees on fixed budgets find themselves unable to absorb skyrocketing premiums.

item.title: Shipping boss pushes for new Bridport channel entrance to stop his ship running aground
item.description: David Harris's ship bottomed out with 400 cattle on board at the end of last year, leading to a 12-hour wait for the next high tide. He wants a solution.

item.title: Disbelief and praise from teenagers and parents as Indonesia's social media ban set to take effect
item.description: In a country with 70 million children, some question how the incoming social media ban in Indonesia will be effectively implemented, while others welcome the new restrictions.

item.title: Private health insurers expand into GP clinics, telehealth, prompting calls for more oversight
item.description: Health insurers increasingly partnering with GP centres and telehealth services has raised concerns that it could create a two-tier system in which privately insured patients have more affordable access to GP consults.

item.title: Why Brumbies skipper Ryan Lonergan is crucial to his side's Super Rugby Pacific championship hopes
item.description: Brumbies captain Ryan Lonergan led by example in the come-from-behind win over the Chiefs, underlining how vital he is to his side's finals hopes.

item.title: Barking dogs, overhanging trees, shared fences — how to resolve issues with your neighbour
item.description: Someone feeling like their neighbour is intruding on them in "either psychological or practical ways" tends to be at the heart of most disputes. So if you're having an issue with the people next door, what's the best way to approach it?

item.title: Donald Trump orders ICE agents to man US airport security checkpoints
item.description: A fight over funding for the Department of Homeland Security leads to lengthy queues at US airports.

item.title: Few leaders in Australia are as firmly entrenched in power as Peter Malinauskas
item.description: After Saturday's landslide, Premier Peter Malinauskas has more political capital than any South Australian Labor leader in modern history.

item.title: Government agency found fuel supply crisis would have 'significant impact' months before Iran war
item.description: Nine months before the Iran war oil crisis, the federal government was warned of the "fragility" of Australia's critical infrastructure if a fuel crisis took hold.

item.title: Queenslanders hit with more than $4 million in fare evasion fines since introduction of 50-cent fares
item.description: The penalties would help authorities recover $7070.50 in revenue they otherwise would have collected if the commuters had paid their 50-cent fare.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
item.description: The ABC's Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran is in Beirut and is providing daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: Supreme Court case highlights how councils use intimidation to coerce homeless people into eviction
item.description: A recent Supreme Court case has shed light on how councils coerce homeless people into eviction.

item.title: Live: Iran threatens to 'completely close' Strait of Hormuz over Trump power plant threat
item.description: Tehran says it is prepared to completely shut down the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route for a large portion of the world's oil, if US President Donald Trump follows through on his vow to "obliterate" them.

item.title: This week in history quiz, March 23
item.description: Can you remember who Will Smith slapped, or what happened during "sandpapergate" scandal? Test yourself with today's quiz.

item.title: Cheryl Grimmer's UK cousin prompts authorities to review decades‑old case
item.description: Michael Grimmer challenges the 2019 court ruling that excluded a key confession in his cousin Cheryl Grimmer's 1970 disappearance, leading to a major breakthrough in the case.

item.title: Live: ASX set to tumble after another brutal night on Wall Street, oil keeps rising
item.description: The prospect of the Middle East war dragging on led Wall Street to fall sharply and oil prices keep rising. The ASX is poised for another sell-off today. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Live: Government lays down ground rules for new data centres
item.description: Australia is home to more than 200 data centres and, as more companies look to build or expand here, the federal government has set out five priorities for them. Follow live.

item.title: How living with a disability in regional Australia poses extra challenges
item.description: NDIS recipients living in regional Australia are calling for the government to provide better care for those who live remotely with travel costs often eating up a large portion of their support budgets.

item.title: The SA election seats to watch as vote counting continues
item.description: ABC chief elections and data analyst Casey Briggs looks at what seats are still in doubt in the South Australian state election, as One Nation makes it into the final two candidates in the count in most of the state's 47 electorates.

item.title: Bret McKenzie reflects on the moment he won an Oscar
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How the historic Lions Den Hotel averted disaster as cyclone spared Cape York
item.description: An intense cyclone approaching the Far North Queensland coast brought back traumatic memories of a daring rooftop helicopter rescue, but with crisis averted, Cooktown is back open for business.

item.title: How a 'mission impossible' Melbourne Cup win is being immortalised in regional Victoria
item.description: In 1948, 15-year-old jockey Ray Neville stunned the nation by winning the Melbourne Cup. Now his story has been immortalised on a trio of silos.

item.title: Jeff Goldblum on singing with Ariana Grande and Cynthia Erivo on Wicked
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: How Sam Kerr, Alanna Kennedy and every other Matildas player fared at the tournament
item.description: From the players who lit up the tournament to those who never quite got going, here is a closer look at how each Matilda fared at the 2026 Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Perth and Mandurah youth homelessness hub Passages sees more demand amid housing crisis
item.description: Service providers like Passages in Perth and Mandurah help hundreds experiencing homelessness during the day, but concern remains amid a shortage of beds across the sector.

item.title: Far North Queensland residents re-open businesses after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Residents in Far North Queensland are returning to their normal lives as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle heads towards the northern coast of Western Australia.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands launches legal action against ARN Media for termination of $100m contract
item.description: Controversial radio host Kyle Sandilands has launched legal action against ARN Media after it terminated his $100 million contract.

item.title: Doomadgee flood isolation to enter fourth month as fuel, food run low
item.description: The Aboriginal Gulf of Carpentaria community of Doomadgee is entering its fourth month of flood isolation, as residents prepare for another month surrounded by rising rivers.

item.title: Jess Hull, Adam Spencer win 1,500m medals as Australia wins five at World Athletics Indoor Championships
item.description: Jess Hull wins her second medal of the world indoor championships, while compatriot Adam Spencer nabs his first global medal as Australia finishes the meet with five medals.

item.title: Underground 'bush doofs' get legal makeover in WA's South West
item.description: Margaret River promoter Muso Kami hopes to ease local concerns and provide a place for people to dance the night away without breaking the law.
